Flame Etched Short Sword  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 17
DMG: 12 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 25
Backstab DMG: 12
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
SV FIRE: +10 SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 3.5 Size: TINY
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 17
DMG: 12 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 25
Backstab DMG: 12
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
SV FIRE: +10 SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 3.5 Size: TINY
Class: ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Stackable:No

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE

IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 08:55:54
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

▲ Page top
yadda yadda yadda
QuoteReply
# Jan 14 2002 at 6:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755344949
39 posts
Awsome poker this is. would definately push my Ragebringer to offhand. With the AC bonus I'd probably have over 1200AC. That, with the nice resists should keep me alive long enough to evade off the agro this gem would build.

Oh for you nit-picky people talking about piercing/flame edge blah blah:
stick this thing into the spleen up to the hilt and there are (gasp) two edges in the flesh you can twist any which way. have fun :)

Yrion Shadowhand
58 thief <Skyre Draneth>
Brell S.

Edited, Mon Jan 14 06:43:57 2002
▲ Page top
Nice
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2002 at 4:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591773642002
View User Forum
15 posts
Great weapon, to say the least. 25 AC? Woot! Go Rogues. :) And stop complaining when Verant releases a piercer that Rogues can't use. :P

As to the weapon discussion above, shortswords were NOT designed as slashing weapons, they were designed as *stabbing* weapons that had a greater reach than a dagger (thus being effective in a fight where reach counts). Of course, you *can* slash with a shortsword, but Verant hasn't made items where you can choose which weapon skill you want to use... so they have to choose one or the other. There are quite a few shortswords in the game... and it seems they go either way. And then there are items like the CSS which shouldn't have been called a Shortsword to begin with... rather a Crystalline Fer`Esh... but hey.

And as for Verant being screwed up on what weapons *should* look like, I think we all knew that the moment we saw any "******* sword" class weapon. The thing looks like a wimpy broad sword, but you swing it in two hands...? For crying out loud, they make *longswords* bigger than ******* swords.

So basically, Verant can do whatever they want to do with items and what skills they use... they've already proven that they really don't care about accuracy (which for most people isn't an issue anyway). In a game where monsters can hit you through walls and you can't hit them, I think they have some more issues besides weapon accuracy.
▲ Page top
Upgraded now
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2001 at 12:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1623025158443
9 posts
I just got this tonight.. the ratio is now :
12/17
truely uber. All the other stats are the same. I put my epic in offhand and this in main.

Stubie, 60 rogue, Saryn

Geesh am I the only warrior in the game that has all his weapon skills maxxed? (ok besides archery, I submit to you that one is impossible to move past 80 for a warrior) It only took me 5 lvls and with taunt I rarely had any problems at all with arrgo on the casters and most groups couldve cared less unless I was the main dmg dealer which is a rare thing for me to do anyway.

You really limit yourslef by not raising all weapon skills. ITs never a smart thing to do.

bodies recieve most devastating damage from flamed edges when slashed, slightly above average damage when stabbed

The tip of a sword comes to a fine point. the edge fo a dagger can be used to slash. It goes both ways, let VI cunfuse us all they want.

War Stories

# Jul 12 2001 at 11:16 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249461149
62 posts
Where do the people that find this stuff swap war stories? I want to hear about that stuff. Who died, who ran, who held the line, who backstabbed and for how much damage? I want to hear about the one that got away, I want pictures dammit! There should be a requirement that if you post a picture, you have to post the brag sheet to go with it.

To the Rogue wielding this; need a lackey? I am a certified Henchman, Bagman and all around Sidekick...

#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 11 2001 at 11:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post)
Bah
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 6:57 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756972840
5 posts
Rapier,
Sabre,
Foils,
Pugious,

All Swords, all designed for PIERCING.
Little or no Edge, but sharp point, and flexible hafts.

Sabres are both, they have an edge and a stabbing point.
▲ Page top
RE: Bah
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2001 at 6:17 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591753111477
45 posts
i agree with you for the most part except for the ALL sowrds are made for stabbing. i dont know how much sword play you have done but try to stab with an katana hehe your going to die =-). also it would be better to swing not stab with all large 2 handed swords for faster attacks =-)
▲ Page top
RE: Bah
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 9:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756015544
Scholar
20 posts
You're right...sabres do have a point. Its not designed to be used tho. A sabre is a very unbalanced weapon and is heavier at the tip than the hilt. It is designed to be used from horseback and primarily as a slashing weapon. The weight at the end is to allow it to carry thru the victim with out effort from the wielder.

FYI, foils are just for sport. There was never a weapon called a foil that was used to fight with.


There are many short swords that have been used as primary piercers...the Roman Gladius comes to mind.

Oh well, enough history..)) Just trying to set a few things straight not cause a flame war.


Ildera Sedai
48th Druid
Tarew Marr
▲ Page top
RE: Bah
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 10:54 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361336744
20 posts
Not trying to be that mentioned flamer (Heaven forbid! Bloody flamers...), but sabres aren't necessarily so unbalanced. Sabres are something of a halfway weapon between the rapier and cutlass, not as heavy and short as the cutlass, not as straight and pointy as the rapier. So, there are some sabres that are lighter, thinner, gentlemen's weapons, not necessarily cavalry sabres. But then sabre is a rather flexible designation. Just trying to defend sabres, love them. Better than any pansie foil!

Talkodar Oakenstave
Druid of Fourteen Rings
Lanys T'Vyl
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 12 2001 at 1:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Why does this matter in anyone's life? Were you so wounded by the comment that you felt you had to "defend sabres"? Yet another in a long line of absurd posts on this site.
Stab?
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 5:54 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750223314
8 posts
I don't understand...how can you stab with something called a sword? Don't all stabbing things have to be called daggers?

I DON'T UNDERSTAND!

Excellent offhander for that Ragebringer.

I doubt the agro would be worth it, but the AC on this definitely helps.

Also, on a raid of this size, I would imagine a rogue can most likely stab away with the tanks that would be slugging it out around him/her.

why name this a short sword if its a peircing weapon?
▲ Page top
RE: sword?
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2001 at 12:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764240175
2 posts
whats with all the conflict? Its simple,a short sword is only a few inches longer than a dagger. They both can stab and they both can slash. Daggers are just shorter, witch are ment to stab. short swords are ment for both. A short sword is the middle man. Next up are the broad swords ment for slahing.
▲ Page top
RE: sword?
QuoteReply
# Oct 28 2001 at 7:38 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756368265
66 posts
Actually, short swords do tend to be stabbing weapons. Someone above mentioned the Roman gladius or drussus, which was almost exclusively a stabbing weapon. Though the Roman legion's formations weren't as tight as the Greek phalanx, there really wasn't enough room to swing wildly around with the sword after the pilum were thrown, or the opponent got inside their reach. Anyway, it don't matter that much. VI could call it Aunt Marny's Overcooked Grits, and I'd wield it with those stats, assuming I ever see one.
▲ Page top
RE: sword?
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 5:40 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
Because it is a SWORD that you STAB with. Understand?
